text: Distributed consensus for multi-agent systems with general linear dynamic models and directed communication topologies This paper deals with the distributed consensus control problem for multi-agent systems with general linear dynamic models and directed communication topologies. Most consensus algorithms depend on the smallest eigenvalue of the graph Laplacian, which is a global parameter. An adaptive distributed consensus protocol that only requires the knowledge of the local dynamic model of each agent and the relative state measurements of its neighbors is then proposed. We show that leader-follower consensus is reached for any communication graph with a directed spanning tree rooted at the leader. As the control law is designed to be independent of the global parameters of the communication graph, it is fully distributed. Finally, the proposed strategy can be easily extended to the case of multi-leaders.
